evidence.text: Benefits benefits benefits benefits benefits Good
Great benefits, lot of time off, Great Benefits!

Benefits, 401k, and PTO are absolutely great. Company name great for resume. Recommend staying for a year or two then bouncing if you donât get promoted. Great stability limited growth, interesting culture
Good Pay, great insurance and time off. Ok

Mars is committed to the well-being of its employees. Some of the most significant benefits of working for Mars include a competitive salary, comprehensive insurance coverage, flexible scheduling, and opportunities for personal and professional growth. Mars is a great place to work
More people-centric than most employers Impossible to get career growth in Data & Analytics, HR (P&O) has FAR TOO MUCH SAY on promotions
Flexible working hours and a lot of senior leadership visibility Analyst

The team is made up of great colleagues who are supportive and collaborative. Compensation, benefits, and RSP contributions are competitive and generous Good Pay, Good People â But Culture Is Declining

Strong brand recognition. Well known name on a resume. Pay and benefits are generally in line with large corporations. This may be a suitable environment for those who prioritize brand name over clarity, fairness, and consistency. Others should proceed with caution. Polished Brand, Dysfunctional Reality
High level of responsibility and autonomy, strong growth opportunities and open and welcoming company culture Great company for growth and development
Good work-life balance and relatively easy work. Decent work-life balance but poor retention
Growth is highly encouraged and work-life balance is emphasized. Great company and work-life balance
Work life balance is great. Great work life balance

One of the Best Company Cultures Iâve experienced. The leadership is transparent, DEI initiatives are real, and they invest in team development. Itâs rare to find a company where everyoneâfrom interns to executives-isâaligned." Excellent Culture and Work-Life Balance
Remote which provides a better work/life balance Great Work Life Balance

Strong brand + structured processes; clear KPIs and reporting. Great exposure to FMCG commercial operations (sales/market execution/supply coordination). Cross-functional teamwork with finance/marketing/supply chain; learned stakeholder management. Friendly environment
Very friendly work environment. Open door policy, easy to reach management. Work environment

evidence.role: Current employee - Strategic Revenue Management Leader
evidence.text: Focus on personal development and career paths, Great work environment and inclusive company where anyone can thrive.
Employer contribution match 401(k), bonuses, good wages/salary, excellent vacation plan, great company overall. Stay away
Very collaborative work environment and friendly colleagues Very good company

evidence.role: Current employee - Head of Tax & Treasury
evidence.text: Free chocolate and fruits, corporate events, business travel, cool office with a massage chair, business travel, fun colleagues, working with people from different parts of the world. Integrity, Speak-Up Culture, and Accountability

Good salary Good work from home flexibility No diversity (Literally no people of colour for a business that says "diversity" is important. No people of colour in the leadership teams) Bullying culture, toxic management and highly political
Pay is decent, job security, Decent Pay
Supportive environment Modern machinery/technologies Project variety Great culture
Not many if you are new. No free chocolates. Procurement

- Toxic Leadership across the executive level. Toxic by creating an environment of fear, stress and incompetence. Leadership is, specifically in the Newark office is inexperienced and self concisious, which creates a toxic environment. - When I was working there, team members were vomiting prior to work, seeing therapists and taking medication just to deal with the situation. - HR did nothing of the situation even a...

Prepare yourself to work with family favorites who have no clue on how to actually make a decision or let alone use a computer. You will be amazed to see how much money the company wastes on sponsoring below average talent who have no legal status, but have connections and murky ties to the business. There is no equity in terms of access to resources. Associates in West Africa are barely acknowledged for their work...

Not fair pay from job to job whatsoever Not worth a long drive 12.5 hour days and nights Poor work schedule rapid swing nights to days Management! Everything about nearly every one of them with that title is arrogant talks down on you disrespectful oppressive No hr or anyone to help from discrimination and harassment on site Very confusing who handles problems with managers if anyone Favoritism Turn over rate Nearly...
Short staffed and favoritism is the worst place to work. Clueless leaders Great company horrible leadership and favoritism
Too much focus on "numbers" that lead to toxic environment. Not enough DVMs. Not enough pay. Bonuses or lack thereof. Leadership being held accountable, or lack thereof. Chaos

Very political, especially at VP level. Nice VPs are rare. Many don't respond if you reach out, which is vastly different vs the lower-ranked employees, who tend to be more open. ZERO career growth in Data & Analytics. Not many roles open up, no automatic promotions or meaningful pay raises, you're just expected to continue in your current role or move "horizontally" without any growth in title or salary for yea...
management wasn't the best, communication across team, career progression if you are already in a senior position Great team with learning opportunities

Bad manager can't remember anything. Not enough chemists, they want you doing the job of 10 people. Make you do manager work and senior level work without promoting you. No growth. Micromanagement, drama, cliquey, terrible pay for the work you do
no jobs promotion in middle and low jobs Good Job, no jobs promotion

-Very Bad Communication From Managers/Coordinators to Technicians -Very Limited Overtime -Need to "Build a Very Good Relationship" with your boss to be considered for a promotion -Expensive and Bad Medical Insurance Was Not Valued
Not spotted any disadvantages there Pet friendly

- Very poor management - Constantly lived harassment from colleagues and leader - Hard push on sales and lack of help from marketing and supporting areas - No digital skills / knowledge and not willing to grow in that area Poor management combined with a tricky work environment
while working for Royal Canin we do not really feel the link with MARS work / life
Toxic environment - all the good people leave due to this Donât work here

evidence.text: I feel like salary is not super competitive, but Iâm might be wrong. Great work life balance
No Work life Balance in Supply Chain Lots of internal Politics Local recourses always dictate terms with outsiders. No Work Life Balance in Supply Chain

no work life balance at mars Excellent Work Culture , higher pay but no work life balance

Burnout culture, fear based culture, extreme internal stakeholdering. Burnout Culture
Poor managers Not many technical thinkers Good benefits
Not competitive with industry salary trends in mid-level positions. Good company with great benefits
Don't have a lot of growth opportunities for lower level roles to move up Decent pay

Extremely numbers driven, with no training or analyst help. Tons of hoops, that prevent you from making a real impact. All internal focused, reporting numbers, tons of meetings etc. If you like Powerpoint, and enjoy running Data in numerous tools you will like Mars. If you want to get things done and see the fruits of your work regularly this may not be the place for you. Good Company. Very Numbers Driven
